text:
Perfect! The HTML structure looks correct:
- All tab panes have the `tab-pane` class
- Only the dashboard tab has the `active` class initially
- Each tab has a unique ID (`tab-dashboard`, `tab-cost`, `tab-appliances`, `tab-rooms`)

The CSS should hide all tab panes except the active one. With the `!important` flag I just added, the CSS rules should definitely take precedence.

Now let me check if the JavaScript is actually executing properly. I can do this by checking if there are any console logs or errors.

Actually, let me think about this differently. The user might be seeing the old cached version of the page. Let me suggest they:1. Clear browser cache and hard refresh (Ctrl+F5)
2. Check the browser console for any JavaScript errors

But wait, I should also verify that the JavaScript file is actually loading correctly and not throwing any errors. Let me check if there are any syntax errors in the JavaScript file by loading it directly.
